title: What Is a Clamp Meter? How It Works and When to Use One
description: In this episode we break down how a clamp meter works — the Hall-effect sensor, the jaw geometry, and why you can measure hundreds of amps without breaking a circuit. We also cover when to reach for a clamp meter versus a standard multimeter, and the common mistakes that give bad readings.
excerpt: A clamp meter measures current without cutting a wire — but the physics behind that trick is worth understanding. We explain Hall-effect sensing, jaw placement, and the real differences from a multimeter.
